name: All That Glitters
description_short: All That Glitters is a FirstCash-affiliated pawn shop in Milwaukie, OR, open 7 days a week offering collateral-based loans and buy/sell services.
description_long:
All That Glitters Pawn Shop is located at 15114 SE McLoughlin Blvd in Milwaukie, Oregon, a suburb in the southeast Portland metro area. The business operates under the FirstCash Holdings umbrella — a NYSE-listed company (ticker: FCFS) and one of the largest pawn operators in the United States and Latin America — with its corporate website listed as firstcash.com. The store serves residents of Milwaukie and surrounding communities along a heavily trafficked commercial corridor.

As a pawn shop, All That Glitters provides collateral-based short-term loans in which customers pledge personal property to secure immediate cash. Standard pawn shop operations also include buying outright and reselling secondhand merchandise. The Waze business listing notes onsite services and credit card acceptance, the latter being a notable amenity not consistently offered by independent pawn operators. The store is open seven days a week, including evenings Monday through Saturday.

Affiliation with FirstCash distinguishes this location from fully independent operators. FirstCash is subject to SEC reporting requirements and operates thousands of locations across the Americas, meaning it carries a higher level of corporate accountability and standardized compliance practices than a standalone shop. The McLoughlin Boulevard address places it on a major arterial with proximity to New Seasons Market, Providence Milwaukie Hospital, and Milwaukie High School, making it accessible to a broad residential and commuter population. Hours of 10am–7pm Monday through Saturday are generous relative to typical pawn shop schedules.

Consumers should understand the fundamental trade-offs inherent to pawn lending: effective annual interest rates on pawn loans are typically far higher than rates offered by credit unions, personal loan lenders, or credit cards. Items pledged as collateral that are not redeemed within the loan term are forfeited and resold. The available public data for this specific location comes from a Waze map listing and does not include disclosed loan rates, accepted item categories, or buyback terms. Anyone facing a cash shortfall should compare pawn loan costs against payday alternatives, credit union emergency loans, or 0% APR credit card offers before pledging valuables.
pros: Open 7 days a week, providing access on any day of the week including Sundays
Extended Monday–Saturday hours (10am–7pm) allow visits before or after a standard workday
Accepts credit cards — uncommon for pawn shops and useful for purchasing resale merchandise
Affiliated with FirstCash Holdings (NYSE: FCFS), a publicly traded company with standardized compliance and consumer protections
High-traffic McLoughlin Blvd location is easily accessible to Milwaukie and southeast Portland residents
Phone number (503-785-1168) listed for quick pre-visit inquiries
cons: Sunday hours are reduced to 11am–5pm only, limiting same-day access on that day
No dedicated local website; all web traffic routes to the corporate FirstCash parent site, with no location-specific details publicly available
Pawn loan interest rates and fee structures are not disclosed in any available public listing for this location
Collateral forfeiture is a real risk — items not redeemed within the loan period are permanently lost to the borrower
Limited publicly verifiable information about accepted item types, minimum loan amounts, or redemption period length specific to this store
best_for: Milwaukie and southeast Portland residents who need immediate cash and own pawnable personal property
Consumers who have been turned down for traditional credit and need a no-credit-check cash option
Bargain shoppers looking for secondhand merchandise at pawn shop resale prices
Sellers looking to liquidate jewelry, electronics, or other valuables quickly
diagnosis: All That Glitters is best for Milwaukie-area residents who need same-day cash and have personal property to pledge as collateral, particularly those ineligible for traditional credit products. The main caveat is that pawn loan costs are typically very high, collateral forfeiture is a real risk if the loan cannot be repaid, and no loan terms for this specific location are publicly available — consumers should exhaust lower-cost alternatives before pawning valuables.
services: Collateral-based pawn loans (personal property pledged for short-term cash)
Merchandise buying (outright purchase of customer items)
Merchandise selling (resale of pawned and purchased goods)
Credit card payment acceptance for purchases
In-person customer service with extended evening hours
Sunday availability for urgent cash needs
